title: START_NOT_STICKY + bounded wake lock is the correct Android service design
description: Changed from START_STICKY (OS restarts service with null intent) to START_NOT_STICKY (OS does not restart). With START_STICKY a null-intent restart ran as an unprovisioned service with no foreground notification — visible as a phantom 'nie relay active' notification. START_NOT_STICKY avoids this; when the user brings the app back the Dart RelayService reconnects and calls start() again. The bounded 1-hour wake lock timeout (was indefinite) is also correct: if onDestroy is somehow skipped the lock expires rather than draining the battery forever. A contributor might suggest reverting to START_STICKY for 'reliability'; this would restore the phantom-notification regression.
